segments.text: In the ninth year, in the tenth month on the tenth day, the word of the Lord came to me:
Son of man, record this date, this very date,
because the king of Babylon has laid siege to Jerusalem this very day.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Tell this rebellious people a parable and say to them:
This is what the Sovereign Lord says: Put on the cooking pot; put it on and pour water into it.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Put into it the pieces of meat, all the choice pieces—the leg and the shoulder. Fill it with the best of these bones;
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Take the pick of the flock. Pile wood beneath it for the bones; bring it to a boil and cook the bones in it.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Woe to the city of bloodshed, to the pot now encrusted, whose deposit will not go away! Take the meat out piece by piece in whatever order it comes.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: For the blood she shed is in her midst: She poured it on the bare rock; she did not pour it on the ground, where the dust would cover it.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: To stir up wrath and take revenge I put her blood on the bare rock, so that it would not be covered.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Woe to the city of bloodshed! I, too, will pile the wood high.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: So heap on the wood and kindle the fire. Cook the meat well, mixing in the spices; and let the bones be charred.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Then set the empty pot on the coals till it becomes hot and its copper glows, so that its impurities may be melted and its deposit burned away.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: It has frustrated all efforts; its heavy deposit has not been removed, not even by fire.
Now your impurity is lewdness. Because I tried to cleanse you but you would not be cleansed from your impurity, you will not be clean again until my wrath against you has subsided.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: I the Lord have spoken. The time has come for me to act. I will not hold back; I will not have pity, nor will I relent. You will be judged according to your conduct and your actions, declares the Sovereign Lord.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: The word of the Lord came to me:
Son of man, with one blow I am about to take away from you the delight of your eyes. Yet do not lament or weep or shed any tears.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Groan quietly; do not mourn for the dead. Keep your turban fastened and your sandals on your feet; do not cover your mustache and beard or eat the customary food of mourners.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So I spoke to the people in the morning, and in the evening my wife died. The next morning I did as I had been commanded.
Won't you tell us what these things have to do with us? Why are you acting like this?
segments.speaker: The People

segments.text: So I said to them,
The word of the Lord came to me:

Say to the people of Israel, This is what the Sovereign Lord says: I am about to desecrate my sanctuary—the stronghold in which you take pride, the delight of your eyes, the object of your affection. The sons and daughters you left behind will fall by the sword.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: And you will do as I have done. You will not cover your mustache and beard or eat the customary food of mourners.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: You will keep your turbans on your heads and your sandals on your feet. You will not mourn or weep but will waste away because of your sins and groan among yourselves.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Ezekiel will be a sign to you; you will do just as he has done. When this happens, you will know that I am the Sovereign Lord.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: And you, son of man, on the day I take away their stronghold, their joy and glory, the delight of their eyes, their heart's desire, and their sons and daughters as well—
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: on that day a fugitive will come to tell you the news.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: At that time your mouth will be opened; you will speak with him and will no longer be silent. So you will be a sign to them, and they will know that I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord
